titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Yale Union Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Staff of Manuscripts and Archives

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday September 4, 2020 at 07:03

did.abstract: The records consist of a constitution, tickets, agreements, a dinner menu, program records, correspondence and memorabilia documenting the Yale Union. Included is material concerning the 1892 debate against the Harvard Union.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 40 (YRG 40), Records of Yale clubs, societies, and organizations.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for materials authored or otherwise produced as official business of Yale University is retained by Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Yale Union Records (RU 78). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged and described at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does no further arrangement and description at the accession level.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of a constitution, tickets, agreements, a dinner menu, program records, correspondence and memorabilia documenting the Yale Union. Included is material concerning the 1892 debate against the Harvard Union.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Yale Union, the first of three Yale student groups with a similar name, was founded in 1920 and disbanded in 1926.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are arranged by subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and memorabilia donated by John Lorance, Yale 1892, relating to the founding of the Yale Union 1891-1895.

did.unittitle: TLS John Lorance to Andrew Keough re gift of papers
Autograph letters to Mr. Lorance from:
James M. Perkins to R.R. Upton
Mr. Simonds, United States Patent Office
Typewritten manuscript entitled "The Renascence of College Debating," by John Lorance

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: A copy of this material is available in digital form from Manuscripts and Archives. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at mssa.assist@yale.edu to request access to the digital copy.

did.unittitle: Constitution and By-laws of the Yale Union
Program of the Joint Debate between the Harvard Union and the Yale Union at Sanders Theatre
Admission ticket to the debate of January 14, 1892
Agreement with the Hyperion Theatre management for use of the theatre
Program of the Joint Debate at the Hyperion Theatre
Admission ticket to the debate of March 25, 1892
Post card from Preston Brown to Mr. Lorance; call for drill
Page of the New York World, January 15, 1892, with account of the first debate between the Harvard and Yale Unions at Cambridge
